questions.question: How do you start a thread?
questions.answer: `thread.start()`

questions.question: What does `thread.join()` do?
options: Waits for the thread to finish before continuing
questions.answer: Waits for the thread to finish before continuing

questions.question: What is a Runnable?
options: A functional interface with `void run()` — defines thread task
questions.answer: A functional interface with `void run()` — defines thread task

questions.question: What does calling `run()` directly (not `start()`) do?
options: Executes run() in the current thread — not in a new thread
questions.answer: Executes run() in the current thread — not in a new thread

questions.question: What is `Thread.sleep(1000)` used for?
questions.answer: Pause current thread for 1000 milliseconds

questions.question: What exception does `Thread.sleep()` throw?
questions.answer: `InterruptedException`

questions.question: Preferred way to create a thread?
questions.answer: Implement Runnable (or use lambda)

questions.question: What is a daemon thread?
options: A background thread — JVM exits when only daemon threads remain
questions.answer: A background thread — JVM exits when only daemon threads remain

questions.question: What is a race condition?
options: Multiple threads accessing shared mutable state without synchronization — unpredictable results
questions.answer: Multiple threads accessing shared mutable state without synchronization — unpredictable results

questions.question: What is a deadlock?
options: Two or more threads waiting for each other to release locks — stuck forever
questions.answer: Two or more threads waiting for each other to release locks — stuck forever

questions.question: What does `synchronized` ensure?
options: Only one thread executes synchronized block at a time
questions.answer: Only one thread executes synchronized block at a time

questions.question: What does `volatile` guarantee?
options: Visibility — all threads see the latest write, but no atomicity for compound ops
questions.answer: Visibility — all threads see the latest write, but no atomicity for compound ops

questions.question: What is an `ExecutorService`?
options: A thread pool manager — submit tasks and reuse threads
questions.answer: A thread pool manager — submit tasks and reuse threads

questions.question: What does `Callable<T>` differ from `Runnable`?
options: Callable can return a value and throw checked exceptions; Runnable cannot
questions.answer: Callable can return a value and throw checked exceptions; Runnable cannot

questions.question: What does `future.get()` do?
options: Blocking wait — returns result when computation finishes, or throws if exception occurred
questions.answer: Blocking wait — returns result when computation finishes, or throws if exception occurred

questions.question: What is `Executors.newCachedThreadPool()`?
options: An elastic pool — creates new threads as needed, reuses idle ones
questions.answer: An elastic pool — creates new threads as needed, reuses idle ones

questions.question: What is `AtomicInteger` used for?
options: Thread-safe integer with atomic operations (getAndIncrement, compareAndSet) — no synchronized needed
questions.answer: Thread-safe integer with atomic operations (getAndIncrement, compareAndSet) — no synchronized needed

questions.question: What is `compareAndSet(expected, update)`?
options: Atomically: sets value to update only if current value equals expected (CAS operation)
questions.answer: Atomically: sets value to update only if current value equals expected (CAS operation)

questions.question: What does `pool.shutdown()` do?
options: Stops accepting new tasks; waits for submitted tasks to complete
questions.answer: Stops accepting new tasks; waits for submitted tasks to complete

questions.question: What is `CountDownLatch` used for?
options: Allow one or more threads to wait until a set of operations completes
questions.answer: Allow one or more threads to wait until a set of operations completes

sublevels.theory:
**`CompletableFuture` (Java 8+):** composable async chains:
```java
CompletableFuture.supplyAsync(() -> fetchData())
    .thenApply(data -> process(data))
    .thenAccept(System.out::println)
    .exceptionally(e -> { log(e); return null; });
```

**`ReentrantLock`:** explicit lock with tryLock, fairness option, condition variables.

**`StampedLock` (Java 8+):** optimistic reads — less overhead for read-heavy workloads.

**Virtual threads (Java 21):** `Thread.ofVirtual().start(runnable)` — millions of lightweight threads.

**Memory model (JMM):** `happens-before` relationship guarantees visibility of writes.

questions.question: What is `CompletableFuture.supplyAsync()` used for?
options: Running a task asynchronously and returning a CompletableFuture for chaining
questions.answer: Running a task asynchronously and returning a CompletableFuture for chaining

questions.question: What does `thenCompose` do in CompletableFuture?
options: Chains a function returning another CF — avoids CF<CF<T>> nesting (flatMap equivalent)
questions.answer: Chains a function returning another CF — avoids CF<CF<T>> nesting (flatMap equivalent)

questions.question: What is `ReentrantLock` vs `synchronized`?
options: ReentrantLock has `tryLock`, interrupt support, fairness option, condition variables — more flexible
questions.answer: ReentrantLock has `tryLock`, interrupt support, fairness option, condition variables — more flexible

questions.question: What is `StampedLock.tryOptimisticRead()`?
options: Read without lock — check stamp afterward to see if write occurred meanwhile
questions.answer: Read without lock — check stamp afterward to see if write occurred meanwhile

questions.question: What are virtual threads (Java 21)?
options: Lightweight JVM-managed threads — millions possible, parked on I/O without blocking OS thread
questions.answer: Lightweight JVM-managed threads — millions possible, parked on I/O without blocking OS thread

questions.question: What is the Java Memory Model (JMM)?
options: Rules for happens-before guarantees — what one thread's writes are visible to another
questions.answer: Rules for happens-before guarantees — what one thread's writes are visible to another

questions.question: What is `Phaser` used for?
options: A reusable synchronization barrier for multiple phases with dynamic party registration
questions.answer: A reusable synchronization barrier for multiple phases with dynamic party registration

questions.question: What does `ForkJoinPool` specialize in?
options: Work-stealing divide-and-conquer parallelism — used by parallel streams
questions.answer: Work-stealing divide-and-conquer parallelism — used by parallel streams

questions.question: What is `ThreadLocal<T>` used for?
options: Per-thread storage — each thread has its own independent copy of the variable
questions.answer: Per-thread storage — each thread has its own independent copy of the variable

questions.question: What is structured concurrency (Java 21 preview)?
options: A model where sub-tasks are scoped to a structured scope — failure cancels all sub-tasks
questions.answer: A model where sub-tasks are scoped to a structured scope — failure cancels all sub-tasks
